review_summary: Required SSH reads were executed against the live backend in this review session: web/app.py, core/services.py, core/models.py, core/permissions.py, backend tests, and backend Python file listing.
Targeted SSH reads confirmed that the live backend exposes GET /api/monitor/live, GET /api/calendar/events, and GET /api/calendar/day?date=YYYY-MM-DD.

Targeted SSH reads also confirmed there is no dedicated mobile-ready dashboard summary endpoint for active bookings / generators out / upcoming returns / pending requests in the currently verified backend routes.
Local Flutter review confirmed lib/features/dashboard/dashboard_screen.dart is still a placeholder and there is no Phase 4 repository/provider/model implementation for monitor or calendar data.

objective: Replace the Phase 4 dashboard placeholder with a real monitor-plus-calendar implementation that matches the live backend contracts exactly, while leaving approved P1-P3 behavior untouched.

behaviors: Do not modify auth restoration, token storage, logout flow, or router auth gating from P1.
Do not modify permanent-generator exclusion, rental_vendor_id handling, or directory classification from P2.
Do not modify booking creation, retailer_out_of_stock handling, merge behavior, bulk update minimum-item enforcement, cancellation, or delete flows from P3.
Do not add Phase 5 billing/history features or Phase 6 admin features.

response_shape.timestamp: ISO8601 UTC string ending in Z

notes: This endpoint is aggregated by date only.
It does not include booking_id, generator_id, or vendor_id.
A calendar tap must therefore drill into day detail by date, not directly to booking detail from the event payload.

phase_scope_correction: Do not invent a dashboard summary endpoint for active bookings / generators out / upcoming returns / pending requests because that route was not verified in this review session.
Phase 4 should implement a monitor-backed dashboard section plus a calendar/date drill-down flow from the verified endpoints above.

required_fixes.what_is_wrong: The screen is a placeholder (`Dashboard Placeholder`) and implements none of the verified Phase 4 backend contracts.
required_fixes.correct_behavior: Replace the placeholder with a real dashboard screen that loads live monitor data from GET /api/monitor/live and live calendar aggregates from GET /api/calendar/events.

required_fixes.what_is_wrong: The dashboard route renders only the placeholder screen; there is no Phase 4 drill-down flow for selected calendar dates.
required_fixes.correct_behavior: Keep `/` routed to the dashboard, but add the internal dashboard flow needed to select a calendar date, fetch GET /api/calendar/day?date=YYYY-MM-DD, and then navigate to the already-approved booking detail route `/bookings/:id` from the returned day data.

required_fixes.what_is_wrong: There is no Flutter code calling the verified Phase 4 routes GET /api/monitor/live, GET /api/calendar/events, or GET /api/calendar/day.
required_fixes.correct_behavior: Add a dashboard repository/provider/model layer that calls exactly those paths with the verified methods and query parameter name `date`.

required_fixes.what_is_wrong: There are no Dart DTOs for the verified monitor and calendar payload shapes, which invites field-name drift and guessed parsing.
required_fixes.correct_behavior: Create Dart models whose JSON keys match the backend exactly: `timestamp`, `cpu.percent`, `cpu.status`, `memory.percent`, `memory.used_mb`, `memory.total_mb`, `memory.status`, `temperature.available`, `temperature.celsius`, `temperature.sensor`, `temperature.status`, `temperature.note`, `title`, `start`, `allDay`, `extendedProps.date`, `vendors`, `vendor_id`, `vendor_name`, `booking_id`, `generator_id`, `capacity_kva`, `start_dt`, `end_dt`, `remarks`.

required_fixes.what_is_wrong: The current implementation cannot match live web behavior because it has no calendar date drill-down, no day detail surface, no booking navigation, no refresh, and no empty/error states.
required_fixes.correct_behavior: Implement pull-to-refresh, last-updated display using monitor `timestamp`, a visible empty state when there are no calendar events, a date-detail surface from `/api/calendar/day`, and tap navigation from a returned `booking_id` to the existing booking detail screen.

optional_improvements: If the UI needs a compact calendar visualization, use a simple monthly grid or date list that stays entirely driven by `/api/calendar/events` and `/api/calendar/day` without inventing unsupported filters.
If monitor access is denied by backend permissions, show a clear gated state for the monitor panel while still allowing calendar data for users who have `read_only_operational_views`.
acceptance_criteria: The dashboard screen no longer contains placeholder text or mocked data.
The app calls GET /api/monitor/live and renders `cpu`, `memory`, and `temperature` using the exact backend field names.
The app calls GET /api/calendar/events and renders date-level calendar entries based on the returned `start` dates.
Selecting a calendar date triggers GET /api/calendar/day` with query parameter `date` in `YYYY-MM-DD` format.
The day-detail UI renders vendors -> bookings -> items using the exact backend keys from `/api/calendar/day`.
A user can tap a returned `booking_id` from day detail and reach the existing booking detail route `/bookings/:id`.
Pull-to-refresh performs fresh network requests for the dashboard data and updates the visible state.
Empty, loading, and error states exist for both monitor and calendar data.
No files outside the allowed scope are modified.

must_not_produce: Prompt or analysis artifacts inside committed source
